page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processing power is delivered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration ensuring dedicated execution resources without hyperthreading.

The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio aligns with compute-intensive profiles. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via external block storage volumes. Baseline network performance is rated at 4 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to its dedicated CPU allocation and 3.2 GHz clock speed, this instance is designed for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared compute performance. The lack of local storage and moderate memory capacity makes it a cost-efficient choice for distributed compute nodes, web serving, microservices, and batch processing tasks where high memory capacity is not a primary requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides dedicated CPU resources with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio optimized for compute-heavy tasks. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as web serving, microservices, and batch processing that demand consistent CPU performance without high memory overhead.
